text:
Data released in structured formats across many domains such as computational biology, information retrieval, and natural language processing is in huge supply. As such, large-scale relational learning is critical to data analysis. In this paper, we propose a neural network model, OpenNE-ML, that could project multi-relational graphs to a continuous vector space with flexible properties, and maintained the intrinsic structure. More importantly, OpenNE-ML could train the model to learn the latent semantic connections that would output the high probability of the corresponding relational components.

Our experimental evaluation shows that our approach performs remarkably well in link prediction tasks over popular datasets and a sample of real world knowledge bases such as WordNet. Finally, we also showcase the scalability and applicability of our model by demonstrating its performance in a specific instantiation of the word-sense disambiguation task, and we demonstrate how our model can be seamlessly integrated into an open-text semantic parsing framework, which is able to assign a structured semantic parse to virtually any free text sentence. This larger experiment allows us to truly measure the scalability of our model by providing experiments on datasets of tens of thousands of examples and thousands of relations.
headline: Fully Human Written
prediction: We believe that this document is fully human-written
